_notes: WP-187 / EC-214 — curated, human-reviewed [effect:] marker map. Consumed by apply-effect-markers.mjs, which appends [effect:<VillainEffectKeyword>] tokens to the matched Ambush:/Fight: ability line in data/cards/*.json. WP-185's engine setup parser reads those markers.

Keys: villains -> setAbbr -> villainGroupSlug -> villainCardSlug -> { ambush?, fight? }; henchmen -> setAbbr -> henchmanGroupSlug -> { ambush?, fight? } (henchman ability text is group-level). Each list holds only the five locked VillainEffectKeyword strings: gainWoundEachPlayer, gainWoundCurrentPlayer, koHeroCurrentPlayer, heroDeckTopToEscape, captureBystander.

v1 curation discipline (WP-185 semantics): a line is marked ONLY when its entire effect reduces to unconditional + magnitude-1 + single-target applications of the MVP vocabulary. Conditional (may / If / '... or ...' choice), magnitude>1 (two / all / 'for each'), wrong/multi target (each villain, the Mastermind, another player, target-restricted KO), and lines carrying an extra non-MVP clause ('... Then ...') are deliberately left unmarked. Conservatism over coverage.

The exhaustive candidate list is reproducible at any time via `node scripts/convert-cards/apply-effect-markers.mjs --propose` (221 phrase-match candidates as of 2026-05-28). The _unassigned block below records the human classification RATIONALE with representative rows per reason; it is intentionally not a 1:1 copy of --propose output.

DATA-vs-SPEC drift found at execution (2026-05-28): (a) The WP assumed two cards (one bkwd, one rvlt) carry two Fight: lines apiece and would loud-fail as reason 'multi-line'. Under the LOCKED predicate (line.trimStart() begins with 'Fight:'), NO card has two timing lines of the same timing — rvlt/army-of-evil/mister-hyde and rvlt/dark-avengers/sentry each have a SECOND 'Fight:' embedded mid-line (inside flavor / a granted ability), which the predicate correctly does not treat as a timing line. So _unassigned carries no 'multi-line' rows; the multi-line loud-fail remains an active guardrail in the script and is exercised via a scratch input. (b) The WP spot-check expected a 'gainWoundEachPlayer' Ambush mark; NO unconditional each-player or current-player wound line exists in any of the 40 sets — every wound line is conditional ('reveal X or gains a Wound', 'If ...', 'with no ...', 'each other player'). So gainWoundEachPlayer and gainWoundCurrentPlayer are uncurated in v1; they await a future conditional/magnitude vocabulary expansion on the WP-185 side.
Escape:/Overrun: timings are handled generically by the script but are uncurated here (WP-186 follow-on).
